steps.prompt: Analyze {target} with scope: {scope}. Scan for issues across quality, security, performance, and maintainability dimensions. Classify each finding as critical, high, medium, or low severity. If any critical findings are discovered, write them to a separate critical-findings.md artifact with detailed descriptions and evidence.

Perform a deep-dive investigation of the critical findings in critical-findings.md for {target}. For each critical finding: trace the root cause, assess the blast radius, determine exploitability or impact severity, identify all related code paths, and document the exact remediation steps required. Prioritize findings by risk.

Implement remediation for the critical findings documented in deep-dive-report.md for {target}. Apply fixes in priority order, ensure each fix is minimal and focused, add regression tests where appropriate, and verify that fixes do not introduce new issues. Document each change made.

Verify the remediation applied to {target}. Re-run the analysis checks against remediation-log.md to confirm all critical findings have been resolved. Run existing tests to ensure no regressions. Document the verification results and any remaining non-critical items.

checkpoint_config.message: Review analysis-report.md and any remediation results. If critical findings were found, deep-dive-report.md and remediation-log.md will also be available. Approve to finalize.
